title: OpenAI's Plan To Merge Chat And Agents — With Greg Brockman
summary: Greg Brockman, OpenAI president and co-founder, argues that compute will ultimately decide the AI race and that there will never be enough compute to satisfy demand. He predicts that interfaces will evolve into persistent agents acting on behalf of users, positioning this as the path toward personal AGI.
writeup: Alex Kantrowitz interviews OpenAI president and co-founder Greg Brockman about how chat interfaces and agents converge. Brockman argues that compute will ultimately decide the AI race, contending that demand will always outstrip supply. He predicts interfaces evolve into persistent agents that act on behalf of users, framing this as the path toward personal AGI.
